name: Germany (state flag)

colors.symbolism: associated with the Holy Roman Empire and the 19th-century pan-German movement
associated with the Hanseatic cities and the Lutzow Free Corps
associated with the imperial eagle of the Holy Roman Empire
the heraldic gold of the federal eagle's beak and claws

description: The black-red-gold horizontal tricolour with the federal eagle shield (Bundesschild) overlaid on the centre.
history: Adopted as the Bundesdienstflagge by the Federal Government on 7 June 1950. Use is restricted by law to federal authorities; private display of this defaced version is prohibited.

features.role: Bundesschild (federal shield), a single-headed black eagle on a yellow shield
features.symbolism: the federal shield marks the flag as for federal authorities only
